From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Sports award given by Punjab state government

**The Maharaja Ranjit Singh Award**, is an [award](/wiki/Award "Award") given by the [Government of Punjab](/wiki/Government_of_Punjab_(India) "Government of Punjab (India)") for excellence and achievement in the service of Punjab in the field of [Sport](/wiki/Sport "Sport"). This can be at the [Olympic](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games") level, [World Championship](/wiki/World_Championship "World Championship") level, national and other international sporting arenas. The award was instituted in 1978 it consists of a trophy of [Maharaja Ranjit Singh](/wiki/Maharaja_Ranjit_Singh_(Punjab) "Maharaja Ranjit Singh (Punjab)"), honouree citation and 5 lakh Rupees. First person in history to win the Maharaja Ranjit Singh Award was Olympian Pargat Singh.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The award was suspended for 10 years between 1996 and 2005 and initiated again in 2006.[[2]](#cite_note-2) However, in 2006 the award was given to individuals where it was backdated from 1997 to 2004 so no potential winner lost their chance to win the award.
